member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.BlindType
member.summary:
A blind / shutter option the customer can pick at configurator Step 7. Pricing is base mounting + per-m² of frame area — the mounting fee covers the bracketry + electrical (if any) and doesn't scale with size; the per-m² part scales with the curtain itself.
separates External (mounted on the façade) from Internal (rolled or pleated behind the frame). External requires solid upper anchor + power feed for electric variants; for now Phase-1 ships external only for window / sliding / panoramic. Compatibility table enforces the matrix.
gates the control choice — manual-only types reject Electric/Remote at the validator. The motor / remote surcharge is applied by on top of the base + per-m² total.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.BlindType.BaseMountingMinor
member.summary: Flat mounting fee in tetri. 18000 = 180 ₾.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.BlindType.SurchargePerSqmMinor
member.summary: Tetri per m² of frame area.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption
member.summary:
A frame color / finish option the customer can pick at configurator Step 6. Color is a configuration-level decision (not per-pane) — the frame is unified across mullions, sashes, and outer trim.
Compatibility is keyed by material via material_color_compatibility: wood laminates only apply to PVC-laminated, RAL Custom is offered to everything, and PVC-white sticks to neutrals. Exactly one row per material is flagged — the configurator auto-selects it on Step 6 entry so the price preview is non-empty.
Per ADR-0002 amendment 2026-05-18 (Step 6): surcharge is flat per order, not per m². Paint match / lamination film prep is largely fixed setup cost; per-m² pricing would punish small windows disproportionately. Roman-locked rates live in the seeder.
is a placeholder slug (ral-custom) — the actual chosen hex + RAL code arrive in the /
fields when the user picks from the RAL palette modal.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.HexCode
member.summary: Hex code (#RRGGBB) used by the FRONT swatch and the 3D scene's frame material. For this is a fallback solid color while the texture loads.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.RalCode
member.summary: Canonical RAL code (e.g. "RAL 9016") for printed/admin reference; null for custom / non-RAL palette entries.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.WoodTextureUrl
member.summary: Public path to the wood-grain texture; required for , null otherwise.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.SurchargeMinor
member.summary: Tetri added once per order; not per-m². 0 for standard colors.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.IsDefault
member.summary: Per-material baseline — typically white-ral9016.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.HexRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match '#'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Fa-f] exactly 6 times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorOption.RalRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^RAL\\s\\d{4}$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match the string "RAL".
○ Match a whitespace character.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 4 times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ColorFamily
member.summary: Family-level grouping used by the FRONT swatch grid. Standard is included at zero surcharge; Premium / WoodLaminate / RalCustom have progressively higher flat surcharges.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.DimensionConstraints
member.summary: Per-product-type allowed dimension range for the Configurator. Sourced from the ProductType row (admin-editable in Phase 2); falls back to a slug-keyed default for unknown / legacy slugs so the calculator can never NPE on a missing range.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.DimensionConstraints.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Per-product-type allowed dimension range for the Configurator. Sourced from the ProductType row (admin-editable in Phase 2); falls back to a slug-keyed default for unknown / legacy slugs so the calculator can never NPE on a missing range.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.DimensionConstraints.ForProductType(System.String)
member.summary: Slug-keyed market-realistic defaults used when seeding new product types and as a safety net when a row hasn't migrated yet. Final numbers are admin-locked per-row; this static map is the floor, not the contract.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.GlassType
member.summary:
A glass package the customer can pick per pane — single/double/triple/quadruple layers, with optional pre-applied treatments (Low-E, tempered, frosted, tinted). Availability is gated by the chosen material via the material_glass_compatibility table — e.g. quadruple-Low-E is only offered with aluminum-thermal profiles that can carry the weight.
One row per material family is flagged so the configurator can auto-select a sensible baseline when the user lands on Step 5 — usually double-standard.
Per ADR-0002 amendment 2026-05-17 (Step 5): is integer tetri above the material baseline; the default package carries 0. Extra treatments applied on top of a glass type (e.g. tempering a double-Low-E pane) live in .

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.GlassType.PaneCount
member.summary: 1..4 — number of glass sheets stacked in the unit.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.GlassType.SurchargePerSqmMinor
member.summary: Tetri / m² added on top of the material baseline. 0 means this is the per-material baseline package. Always integer; decimal only in the calculator (per ADR-0002).

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.GlassType.UValue
member.summary: Thermal transmittance — W/(m²·K). Informational, used in marketing copy on the Step 5 card. Lower = better insulation.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.GlassType.IsDefault
member.summary: True for the per-material baseline. Exactly one row per material in material_glass_compatibility should be flagged default — the reader resolves it for the configurator's auto-select.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.HandleStyle
member.summary:
A handle option the customer can pick at configurator Step 7. Surcharge is per openable pane — a 2-pane Casement+Fixed window with the "modern-aluminum" handle pays 1× the per-pane surcharge (only the Casement pane gets a handle).
Family is a string enum kept loose (we don't carry an enum on the wire; it shapes FRONT swatch grouping only). Compatibility with materials is modeled via material_handle_compatibility — most handles work across all materials; rare cases (e.g. premium-secustic) are ALU-only.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.HandleStyle.Family
member.summary: "modern" | "classic" | "premium" | "minimal".

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.HandleStyle.ImageUrl
member.summary: Public path to the swatch / hero image; null until Phase 1.5.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.HandleStyle.SurchargePerPaneMinor
member.summary: Tetri per openable pane. 4500 = 45 ₾/pane.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.HandleStyle.IsDefault
member.summary: One default per family — Step-7 auto-select fallback.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.CatalogSlugRules
member.summary: Shared catalog slug validator — lowercase ASCII letters / digits / hyphens, 2-64 characters, no leading/trailing hyphen. Used by every catalog entity introduced from Step 5 onwards.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.LockType
member.summary:
A lock option the customer can pick at configurator Step 7. Surcharge is per openable pane (each handle gets its own lock cylinder).
is the engineering constraint: a multi-point lock needs a full-perimeter opening to anchor the locking points around. A Tilt-only or Sliding-only configuration can't carry one — the validator rejects that pairing with metadata pointing at the offending lock slug.
Phase-1 scope: is door-only (the smart fingerprint vendor we're integrating with hasn't shipped window firmware). Compatibility table enforces it.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.LockType.SecurityRating
member.summary: 1..5 stars displayed by the FRONT.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.LockType.RequiresCasementOrTurn
member.summary: True for multi-point locks that physically need a Casement or TiltAndTurn pane. The validator surfaces configurator.accessory.lockRequiresFullOpening when the chosen layout has no such pane.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.Material.BasePricePerSqmMinor
member.summary: Storage representation: int minor units (tetri for GEL). 38000 = 380.00 ₾/m². Never a decimal/double — see ADR-0002. Convert via at display / DTO boundaries.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ProductType.WarrantyMonths
member.summary: Frame warranty in months — surfaced at Step 8 and on the post-order confirmation. Roman-locked per product family (window 36, door 60, sliding 36, panoramic 36, balcony 24). Phase 1 admin tooling will move this to a per-row editable value.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ProductType.LeadTimeDaysMin
member.summary: Base production lead time (low end). Modified by the configuration via .

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ProductType.LeadTimeDaysMax
member.summary: Base production lead time (high end).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Catalog.ProductType.GetConstraints
member.summary: Resolves the dimension constraints from the entity's own columns. Falls back to the slug-keyed defaults when columns are zeroed (e.g. rows that pre-dated the AddDimensionConstraints migration and somehow escaped the seeder backfill).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Error
member.summary: Domain error. is dot-namespaced English (machine-readable, stable); is Georgian (user-facing, server-localized). is non-null when the error is bound to an input property — populated by FluentValidation. carries structured context the UI uses to render the error precisely (min/max/actual on out-of-range, expected/got on mismatch, etc.).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Error.#ctor(System.String,System.String,BEQSAN.Domain.Common.ErrorType,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Domain error. is dot-namespaced English (machine-readable, stable); is Georgian (user-facing, server-localized). is non-null when the error is bound to an input property — populated by FluentValidation. carries structured context the UI uses to render the error precisely (min/max/actual on out-of-range, expected/got on mismatch, etc.).

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Error.WithMetadata(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Returns a new Error with one entry appended (or overwritten) in the metadata bag. The record stays immutable; existing references to the original Error are unaffected.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Result.Errors
member.summary: All errors collected for this failure. Empty when is true. Typical sizes: 0 (success), 1 (single error), 2+ (validation across multiple fields).

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Common.Result.Error
member.summary: Convenience accessor: first error in , or on success. Tests and most handler call-sites only care about the primary error.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.AccessorySelection
member.summary:
Configuration-level accessory bundle. Every field is optional — the customer can pick any subset of (handle, lock, sill, blind) for their configuration. Mosquito net is per-pane (see ), not in here.
Handle + lock prices scale with the number of openable panes (per-pane surcharge × count). Sill is config-level (one continuous slab below the frame) priced by linear metres. Blind is a single assembly per frame, priced as base mounting + per-m² + control surcharge.
Door product types have required handle + lock (legal + security expectation) — the validator enforces this when any openable pane is present. Window / sliding / etc. treat the whole selection as optional.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.AccessorySelection.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.Nullable{System.Guid},BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.SillSelection,BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.BlindSelection)
member.summary:
Configuration-level accessory bundle. Every field is optional — the customer can pick any subset of (handle, lock, sill, blind) for their configuration. Mosquito net is per-pane (see ), not in here.
Handle + lock prices scale with the number of openable panes (per-pane surcharge × count). Sill is config-level (one continuous slab below the frame) priced by linear metres. Blind is a single assembly per frame, priced as base mounting + per-m² + control surcharge.
Door product types have required handle + lock (legal + security expectation) — the validator enforces this when any openable pane is present. Window / sliding / etc. treat the whole selection as optional.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.AccessoryValidator
member.summary:
Pure validation of an against the chosen product type, material, and pane layout. Split out from at the Step-7 size threshold — same shape, same "no I/O, no time, no randomness" contract.
Caller hands in an bag with the per-material / per-product-type allow-lists already resolved by the pricing handler. Returns when the selection is null (whole step is optional) OR every present field passes its compatibility + per-product rules.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.AccessoryCatalog
member.summary: Lookup bag passed in by the pricing handler. Holds per-material / per-product-type allow-lists + the domain entities the validator + calculator need. Built once per request; immutable.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.ColorSelection
member.summary:
Configuration-level color decision. Frame color is unified across all panes — per-pane color was deliberately excluded from the Step 6 scope (mixed-frame-color windows are non-standard).
is the visible-outside color and is always required. is optional — when null, "inner = outer" (the typical case). Only PVC frames support a different inner color; aluminum is single-pass painted/anodized so inner ≠ outer is rejected by the layout validator.
When the outer slug is ral-custom, and carry the modal-picked values and are both required. For any other selection they should be null.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.ColorSelection.#ctor(System.Guid,System.Nullable{System.Guid},System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Configuration-level color decision. Frame color is unified across all panes — per-pane color was deliberately excluded from the Step 6 scope (mixed-frame-color windows are non-standard).
is the visible-outside color and is always required. is optional — when null, "inner = outer" (the typical case). Only PVC frames support a different inner color; aluminum is single-pass painted/anodized so inner ≠ outer is rejected by the layout validator.
When the outer slug is ral-custom, and carry the modal-picked values and are both required. For any other selection they should be null.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.ConfigurationPane
member.summary:
A single pane in a multi-pane layout. Value-object record — equality is structural so configurations can be compared and de-duped.
is 1-based, left-to-right. is 0..1; LayoutValidator enforces that ratios across panes sum to 1.0 within a 0.001 tolerance. is required for Casement and TiltAndTurn openings, forbidden for Fixed / Tilt / Sliding. is a per-pane accessory toggle pricing-wise; the full accessory catalog lands in Step 7. picks one of the GlassType packages compatible with the chosen material. means "let the server pick the default for this material" — the backcompat path that keeps canaries #1, #2, #3 valid (they default to double-standard). are additive per-pane treatments on top of the glass type. Frosted + Tinted is rejected by the validator. Null is treated as empty by so consumers don't have to coalesce manually.
The last two params default so Steps 1-4 call sites that never knew about glass still construct valid panes — pass (Position, WidthRatio, OpeningType, HingeSide, HasMosquitoNet) and the calculator resolves the rest from the material's default glass package.

A single pane in a multi-pane layout. Value-object record — equality is structural so configurations can be compared and de-duped.
is 1-based, left-to-right. is 0..1; LayoutValidator enforces that ratios across panes sum to 1.0 within a 0.001 tolerance. is required for Casement and TiltAndTurn openings, forbidden for Fixed / Tilt / Sliding. is a per-pane accessory toggle pricing-wise; the full accessory catalog lands in Step 7. picks one of the GlassType packages compatible with the chosen material. means "let the server pick the default for this material" — the backcompat path that keeps canaries #1, #2, #3 valid (they default to double-standard). are additive per-pane treatments on top of the glass type. Frosted + Tinted is rejected by the validator. Null is treated as empty by so consumers don't have to coalesce manually.
The last two params default so Steps 1-4 call sites that never knew about glass still construct valid panes — pass (Position, WidthRatio, OpeningType, HingeSide, HasMosquitoNet) and the calculator resolves the rest from the material's default glass package.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.ConfigurationPane.Extras
member.summary: Null-safe accessor for the extras list — consumers iterate this instead of directly so a pane built from the 5-arg backcompat constructor doesn't crash on NPE.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtra
member.summary:
Per-pane additive treatments layered on top of the chosen GlassType. They stack — a customer can pick "triple-low-e" as the type and still ask for tempered + tinted on the pane in front of a balcony.
Two treatments are mutually exclusive on the same pane: and — one obscures, the other shades; combining them serves no visible purpose and the validator rejects it.
Pricing per m² of pane area is in . Phase 2 moves these to an admin-editable PricingRule entity; for now Roman locks the rates in code (see ADR-0002 amendment for Step 5).

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtra.LowECoating
member.summary: ენერგო-ეფექტური საფარი — heat-reflective coating.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtra.Tempered
member.summary: დაკაჟებული — safety-tempered glass.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtra.Frosted
member.summary: მქრქალი — etched / sandblasted for privacy.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtra.Tinted
member.summary: ტონირებული — bronze / grey tint, reduces solar gain.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.GlassExtraPricing
member.summary: Pure pricing table for glass extras. Per-pane surcharge in tetri per square metre of pane area. Roman-locked numbers; admin-editable in Phase 2 via the PricingRule entity.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationOption
member.summary:
Configuration-level installation decision picked at Step 8. Pricing is zone-based (Roman-locked) for Phase 1 — per-km dynamic pricing is Phase-2 work if/when real demand justifies it.
drives the surcharge via . Batumi is free (within ~30 km radius), Other → we quote manually after a phone call and surface that intent via .
is an optional free-text breadcrumb for the crew — "Batumi, Khimshiashvili 7" — kept null when the customer hasn't typed anything; only meaningful when paired with .

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationOption.#ctor(BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Configuration-level installation decision picked at Step 8. Pricing is zone-based (Roman-locked) for Phase 1 — per-km dynamic pricing is Phase-2 work if/when real demand justifies it.
drives the surcharge via . Batumi is free (within ~30 km radius), Other → we quote manually after a phone call and surface that intent via .
is an optional free-text breadcrumb for the crew — "Batumi, Khimshiashvili 7" — kept null when the customer hasn't typed anything; only meaningful when paired with .

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.Batumi
member.summary: Batumi + 30 km — free.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.KobuletiCoast
member.summary: Kobuleti, Khelvachauri, Khulo — +100 ₾.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.Guria
member.summary: Ozurgeti, Lanchkhuti, Chokhatauri — +150 ₾.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.Imereti
member.summary: Kutaisi, Tskaltubo, Samtredia — +220 ₾.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.Samegrelo
member.summary: Zugdidi, Senaki, Poti — +280 ₾.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.EastGeorgia
member.summary: Tbilisi, Rustavi, anything not on the coast — +400 ₾.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion.Other
member.summary: Anything else — Roman calls within an hour and quotes manually.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationPricing
M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationPricing.RequiresManualQuote(BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.InstallationRegion)
member.summary: True when the chosen region falls outside the standard zone table and Roman handles the quote on the phone. The still emits a zero-amount line so the FRONT can render the "we'll call you" affordance.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.LayoutValidator
member.summary:
Pure validation of a multi-pane layout against a product type. No I/O, no time, no randomness — same inputs always produce the same Result. Used by before any pricing math runs.
Rules enforced (in this order, first failure wins so errors stay specific): 1. Pane count within slug-keyed [min, max] band. 2. Σ WidthRatio = 1.0 within 0.001 tolerance. 3. Positions form a complete 1..N sequence (no gaps, no duplicates). 4. Slug-specific rules: door — at most one Fixed pane (sidelight) sliding — every pane must be Sliding or Fixed 5. Hinge side matrix: Casement / TiltAndTurn → HingeSide required Fixed / Tilt / Sliding → HingeSide forbidden 6. Glass rules (only when an availableGlassTypes set is supplied): - Each pane's GlassTypeId must be a key in the available set. - Frosted + Tinted may not coexist on a single pane. 7. Color rules (only when a colorSelection is supplied): - Outer color must be a key in the availableColorOptions set. - Inner color (when supplied) must also be in the set. - Dual-color (inner ≠ outer) is PVC-only. - When the outer slug is ral-custom: CustomRalHex + CustomRalCode must both be supplied and well-formed. The optional params let Step 1-N call sites that don't yet know about a rule family bypass it without breaking — every existing canary keeps its number when called with null/omitted args.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.LayoutValidator.PaneCountRange(System.String)
member.summary: Slug-keyed pane-count band. ProductType.MinPaneCount / MaxPaneCount columns are Phase 2 admin work; until then the static table here is the source of truth, mirrored on the FRONT for the segmented control.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.LeadTimeEstimator
member.summary:
Pure production-and-install lead-time estimator. No I/O, no clock, no randomness — same inputs always produce the same window. The result is a min/max pair because real workshops give ranges; the FRONT renders "{min}–{max} days" on Step 8.
Formula (Phase 1 — Roman-validated against shop capacity):
Start with + base window.
+ pane-count factor: 2 panes → +1 day, 3 → +3, 4+ → +5.
+ blind (motor mount, electrical run): +2/+3 days on the window.
+ smart-lock (vendor lead, wiring): +3/+5 days.
+ regional install days (Batumi 1 / nearby zones 2 / mid 3 / east 4).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType
member.summary: How a single pane opens. Surcharge rates baked into — admin-editable per-product-type pricing matrix is Phase 2 territory.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType.Fixed
member.summary: ყრუ — fixed glass, no opening mechanism. No surcharge.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType.Casement
member.summary: გასაღები — single-axis swing (casement). +8%.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType.Tilt
member.summary: დასაკეცი — top-pivot tilt. +10%.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType.TiltAndTurn
member.summary: გასაღები + დასაკეცი — combined casement + tilt. +18%.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PaneOpeningType.Sliding
member.summary: სლაიდინგი — horizontal slider. +12%. Only valid for sliding/balcony product types.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceBreakdown
M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceBreakdown.#ctor(System.Decimal,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceLine},System.Int64,System.String)
T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator
member.summary:
Pure pricing function — no I/O, no time, no randomness. Same inputs always produce the same breakdown.
Per ADR-0002: money is int minor units (tetri) at storage and transport; decimal lives only in this calculator. Banker's rounding everywhere so 0.5-tetri cases don't drift up systematically.
Composition (Step 5): material_minor = round(area × material.basePricePerSqmMinor) for each pane p with paneArea = area × p.widthRatio: opening_minor += round(paneArea × material.basePricePerSqmMinor × SurchargeRate(p.openingType)) → line "pane.{n}.opening.{type}" if > 0 glass_minor += round(paneArea × glassType.SurchargePerSqmMinor) → line "pane.{n}.glass.{slug}" if > 0 for each distinct extra: extra_minor += round(paneArea × GlassExtraPricing.SurchargeMinor(extra)) → line "pane.{n}.glass.extra.{name}" mosquito_minor = 8000 tetri × #(panes with HasMosquitoNet) subtotal_minor = material + opening + glass + extras + mosquito vat_minor = round(subtotal × 0.18) total_minor = subtotal + vat
Backwards compat: - When panes is null or empty, a single full-width Fixed pane is synthesized. Canaries #1 / #2 hold (753.31 / 832.61 GEL). - When availableGlassTypes is null/empty, all glass math is skipped. Canary #3 holds (1077.23 GEL) because the Step 4 commit was written before glass landed and its tests don't pass a glass set. - When availableGlassTypes is supplied, panes with == are auto-resolved to the IsDefault glass type for that material so callers don't need to know the material's default themselves.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.MosquitoNetMinor
member.summary: Per-pane mosquito-net flat surcharge in tetri. Phase-2 accessory entity replaces.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.TransomMullionMinor
member.summary: Flat hardware cost in tetri added per pane that has a transom (horizontal frame split). Covers the extra mullion + seal + installation labor. Phase-2 admin pricing promotes this to a PricingRule row.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.DismantlingPerPaneMinor
member.summary: Flat dismantling cost per pane in tetri (30 GEL).

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.LiftElevatorFlatMinor
member.summary: Flat carrying/lifting fee in tetri if elevator is present for apartments (20 GEL).

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.LiftStairsPerPanePerFloorMinor
member.summary: Carrying fee per pane per floor (above floor 1) in tetri if stairs only (5 GEL).

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.InnerColorRate
member.summary: Inner-color surcharge applied at 60% of the inner option's flat surcharge — dual-color is cheaper than two full paint jobs because only the inner face is reworked from the baseline white.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.SillPerMeterMinor
member.summary: Sill per-meter base rate in tetri (8 000 = 80 ₾/m). Phase-2 admin tooling promotes this to a PricingRule row.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.BlindElectricControlSurchargeMinor
member.summary: Tetri added on top of a blind's base + per-m² when the requested control is Electric.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceCalculator.BlindRemoteControlSurchargeMinor
member.summary: Tetri added when the requested control is Remote (motor + handheld). Higher than Electric because the remote bundle includes a paired transmitter.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceLine
member.summary: One line in a price breakdown. Code is machine-readable English (matches docs/api/result-envelope.md code taxonomy: "material", "vat", "glass", "color", "accessories"); label is Georgian for direct display. AmountMinor is tetri/cents — never decimal in storage or transport.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.PriceLine.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: One line in a price breakdown. Code is machine-readable English (matches docs/api/result-envelope.md code taxonomy: "material", "vat", "glass", "color", "accessories"); label is Georgian for direct display. AmountMinor is tetri/cents — never decimal in storage or transport.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.WarrantyEstimator
member.summary: Maps a finished configuration to its warranty terms. The base months come from ; certain premium accessories add vendor-backed notes the FRONT surfaces below the headline number.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Configurator.WarrantyEstimator.SmartLockVendorWarrantyMonths
member.summary: Smart-lock manufacturer's own warranty layered on top of the BEQSAN frame warranty. The caller of resolves hasSmartLock from LockGrade.Smart in the accessory selection.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.EncryptedToken
member.summary: Symmetric-encrypted token at rest. Keep + together — the cipher is meaningless without its IV. Plaintext is never stored nor logged; decryption happens at the edge of the Meta HTTP call site only.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.EncryptedToken.#ctor(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.DateTime)
member.summary: Symmetric-encrypted token at rest. Keep + together — the cipher is meaningless without its IV. Plaintext is never stored nor logged; decryption happens at the edge of the Meta HTTP call site only.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxMessage
member.summary: One message inside an . For comment-channel threads, each is a comment (top-level or reply) on the linked post. Meta-side id stays unique-per-page.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxChannel.InstagramDm
member.summary: Instagram direct message conversation.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxChannel.FacebookMessenger
member.summary: Facebook Messenger conversation.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxChannel.InstagramComment
member.summary: Comment thread under an Instagram post.

member.name: F:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxChannel.FacebookComment
member.summary: Comment thread under a Facebook post.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxThread
member.summary: One conversation. For DMs that's a Messenger thread; for comments it's the per-post comment list (we group all top-level comments + their replies into a single thread keyed on the post). Last-message preview + unread flag are denormalized off the message stream for cheap list rendering.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxThread.ExternalThreadId
member.summary: Meta-side stable id for the thread (conversation id or post id).

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.InboxThread.ParticipantId
member.summary: The customer's Meta-side user id (PSID or IGSID).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialAccount
member.summary: Meta user-level account connected by the workspace owner. Holds the long-lived user access token. Pages and IG business accounts hang off this via . A workspace usually has 1-2 (one personal, one business). Soft-delete via — we keep historic posts after a disconnect so audit lookups still work.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialAccount.MetaUserId
member.summary: Meta user id (numeric string returned by /me).

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialAccount.DisplayName
member.summary: Display name returned by /me.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialAccount.UserToken
member.summary: Encrypted long-lived user access token.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialPage
member.summary: A Facebook Page (and its optional Instagram business account) connected via . The page-scoped long-lived token is what we use for all publish/inbox/comment calls — never the user token. is the Instagram business account id; if null, IG publishing is disabled for this page.

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialPage.MetaPageId
member.summary: Meta page id (numeric string).

member.name: P:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialPage.PageToken
member.summary: Encrypted page-scoped long-lived access token.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialPlatform
member.summary: Outbound publishing target and inbox source platform. Stored as string in DB.

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.Social.SocialPost
member.summary: Outbound publish record. One row per (page, platform) target — a single composer submission that fans out to both FB + IG produces two rows that share a . is the Meta id we get back after publish (used to link comments + insights later).

member.name: T:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText
member.summary: Translated string with Georgian as the always-required primary and English/Russian optional. Stored as JSON in SQLite (single TEXT column). The shape mirrors the FRONT i18next contract: if en/ru is null, the UI falls back to ka.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.LocalizedText.#ctor
member.summary: Parameterless constructor for System.Text.Json. Use for production code.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.Money.FromMinor(System.Int64,BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.Currency)
member.summary: Build a Money value from minor units (tetri for GEL, cents for USD/EUR). 63840 → 638.40 ₾. Use this whenever crossing the persistence boundary — pricing storage is int minor-units only, see ADR-0002.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.Money.ToMinor
member.summary: Project to int minor units for storage / wire transport. Uses banker's rounding so 0.5 tetri sums don't drift up systematically.

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.PhoneNumber.NonDigitOrPlus
member.remarks:
Pattern:
[^\\d+]
Options:
RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match a character in the set [^+\d].

member.name: M:BEQSAN.Domain.ValueObjects.PhoneNumber.GeorgianMobile
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^\\+9955\\d{8}$
Options:
RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match the string "+9955".
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 8 times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0
member.summary: Custom -derived type for the HexRegex method.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.Instance
member.summary: Cached, thread-safe singleton instance.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes the instance.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory
member.summary: Provides a factory for creating instances to be used by methods on .

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory.CreateInstance
member.summary: Creates an instance of a used by methods on .

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner
member.summary: Provides the runner that contains the custom logic implementing the specified regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.Scan(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Scan the starting from base.runtextstart for the next match.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryFindNextPossibleStartingPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Search starting from base.runtextpos for the next location a match could possibly start.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if a possible match was found; false if no more matches are possible.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.HexRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryMatchAtCurrentPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Determine whether at base.runtextpos is a match for the regular expression.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if the regular expression matches at the current position; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities
member.summary: Helper methods used by generated -derived implementations.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_defaultTimeout
member.summary: Default timeout value set in , or if none was set.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_hasTimeout
member.summary: Whether is non-infinite.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.IndexOfNonAsciiOrAny_AD4D819CE816ED030B95777612CA443662191CF6908E9E44CA79CD9A7619053C(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Finds the next index of any character that matches a character in the set [^+\d].

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_ascii_8FF030000000000000000
member.summary: Supports searching for characters in or not in "+0123456789".
